skill.name: Mobile Developer
skill.description: Develop React Native, Flutter, or native mobile apps with modern architecture patterns
skill.summary: Cross-platform mobile development expert for React Native, Flutter, and native iOS/Android with performance optimization and app store deployment
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains only prompt instructions with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 security issues. The skill provides mobile development guidance for React Native, Flutter, and native platforms without any network access, filesystem operations, or external command execution. Safe for publication.

content.user_title: Build cross-platform mobile apps with React Native and Flutter
content.value_statement: Mobile developers struggle with cross-platform consistency and native integrations. This skill provides expert guidance for React Native, Flutter, and native development with modern architecture patterns.
actual_capabilities: Architect cross-platform apps using React Native New Architecture and Flutter 3.x
Implement native modules with Swift for iOS and Kotlin for Android
Optimize mobile performance for 60fps animations and fast cold launches
Set up CI/CD pipelines with Fastlane, Bitrise, and GitHub Actions
Design offline-first data sync with conflict resolution strategies
Integrate platform services including push notifications, deep linking, and payments
limitations: Cannot execute code or run mobile simulators directly
Cannot access App Store Connect or Google Play Console accounts
Cannot test on physical devices or device farms
Cannot perform actual code signing or certificate management

use_cases.title: Cross-Platform E-Commerce App
use_cases.description: Build a React Native e-commerce app with offline cart, payment integration, and push notifications for order updates
use_cases.target_user: Startup CTO building MVP with limited engineering resources

use_cases.title: Flutter Enterprise Migration
use_cases.description: Migrate legacy Xamarin app to Flutter with Material 3 design, local database, and enterprise authentication
use_cases.target_user: Enterprise developer modernizing existing mobile applications

use_cases.title: Native Feature Integration
use_cases.description: Create native modules for camera processing, biometric auth, and BLE connectivity in cross-platform apps
use_cases.target_user: Mobile engineer adding platform-specific hardware features

prompt_templates.title: Basic Mobile App Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new React Native app with TypeScript, navigation setup, and folder structure following best practices. Include instructions for running on iOS and Android simulators.

prompt_templates.title: State Management Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement Redux Toolkit in my React Native app for managing user authentication state, shopping cart, and API caching. Include folder structure and store configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Native Module Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through creating a native iOS module in Swift for React Native that accesses the camera for document scanning with edge detection. Include the bridging code.

prompt_templates.title: Performance Optimization Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Flutter app architecture and identify performance bottlenecks. Provide specific recommendations for improving startup time, memory usage, and maintaining 60fps during scroll and animations.

output_examples.input: Set up React Native project with navigation
output: 1. Initialize project: npx react-native init MyApp --template react-native-template-typescript
3. Install dependencies: npm install react-native-screens react-native-safe-area-context
4. Create navigation structure with Stack.Navigator and Screen components
5. Configure deep linking in AndroidManifest.xml and Info.plist

output_examples.input: Implement offline sync for Flutter app
output: Use Hive or Isar for local database storage
Implement Repository pattern with local and remote data sources
Create sync service with connectivity listener using connectivity_plus package
Handle conflict resolution using last-write-wins or vector clocks
Queue mutations for retry when connection restored

best_practices: Follow platform-specific design guidelines: Human Interface Guidelines for iOS and Material Design for Android
Implement proper error boundaries and graceful degradation for offline scenarios
Use environment variables for API endpoints and never commit sensitive configuration to version control
anti_patterns: Storing API keys or secrets directly in app code instead of using secure backend services
Making synchronous network calls on the main thread causing UI freezes
Ignoring platform-specific accessibility features and touch target sizes

faq.question: Should I choose React Native or Flutter for my project?
faq.answer: Choose React Native if you have JavaScript expertise or need native module integration. Choose Flutter for consistent UI across platforms and superior performance. Both are production-ready with strong ecosystems.

faq.question: How do I handle different screen sizes and densities?
faq.answer: Use responsive design with percentage-based layouts, platform-specific assets, and libraries like react-native-responsive-dimensions or Flutter's LayoutBuilder. Test on multiple device sizes during development.

faq.question: What is the typical app review timeline for iOS and Android?
faq.answer: iOS App Store review typically takes 24-48 hours. Google Play review ranges from a few hours to 2 days for new apps. Build in buffer time for potential rejections and resubmissions.

faq.question: How do I implement biometric authentication?
faq.answer: Use react-native-biometrics or expo-local-authentication for React Native. For Flutter, use local_auth package. Always provide fallback authentication and handle platform-specific permission flows.

faq.question: What is the best approach for offline-first mobile apps?
faq.answer: Implement local database (SQLite, Realm, Hive), queue mutations for sync, use optimistic UI updates, and handle conflict resolution. Consider using libraries like WatermelonDB or RxDB for reactive sync.

faq.question: How do I reduce mobile app bundle size?
faq.answer: Enable ProGuard/R8 for Android, use app thinning for iOS, lazy load features, optimize images with WebP format, remove unused code and assets, and consider modular architecture for large apps.
